titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Beinecke Library Construction Photographs
titlestmt.author: by Michael Rush

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 19 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 529 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 17, 2025 at 23:00

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library
Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Beinecke Library construction photographs are arranged in rough chronological order.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Beinecke Library Construction Photographs is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The Beinecke Library accrued the Beinecke Library construction photographs at the time of the construction of the libary between 1960 and 1963.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Beinecke Library Construction Photographs. Beinecke Rare Book and Manscript Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Yale University built the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library between 1960 and 1963. Architect Gordon Bunshaft of the firm Skidmore, Owings & Merrill designed the building. The George A. Fuller Construction Company was the general contractor. The building and its endowment are the gift of Edwin J. Beinecke, Yale 1907; Frederick W. Beinecke, Yale 1909; Walter Beinecke, Yale 1910; and their families.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The Beinecke Library construction photographs were initially described by Kathi Isham as part of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Records (BRBL RECORD GROUP 1). In 2025 Michael Rush separated the construction photographs from the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Records to facilitate continued public access to the photographs. At that time he made adjustments to the arrangement and description.

Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Beinecke Library construction photographs consist of mostly black and white, with some color, photographic prints documenting the construction of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library between 1960 and 1963. The photographs capture exterior and interior shots of the construction process, as well as images of the finished building, the movement of collections from Sterling Memorial Library, the opening rececption, and notable contributors to the creation of the library including University Librarian James Babb, inaugural Beinecke Library Director Herman W. "Fritz" Liebert, Edwin Beinecke, Frederick Beinecke, and sculptor Isamu Noguchi. The photographer is unknown for most prints - a small group of photographs were taken by C. T. Alburtus.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Records (BRBL Record Group 1)
Related material: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University, photographs (RU 106). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.
